title: stylidium involucratum
intro: Stylidium involucratum, also known as the button wrack or button triggerplant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Stylidium involucratum include its ability to aid in the healing of cuts and scrapes, reduce inflammation and pain, and promote the growth of new tissue. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tincture or infusion, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
